title: [workflowautomation] fix: add build context to telegram-bot service so docker-compose builds from source on VPS
rule_summary:
**What was fixed**  
The `telegram-bot` service in `docker-compose.yml` was missing a `build` context, causing Docker Compose to fail when building from source on a VPS.

**Why it broke**  
Without an
lesson_summary:
**What was fixed**  
The `telegram-bot` service in `docker-compose.yml` was missing a `build` context, causing Docker Compose to fail when building from source on a VPS.

**Why it broke**  
Without an explicit build context, Docker Compose defaults to the current directory or uses a prebuilt image.

title: [workflowautomation] fix: use POSIX '.' instead of bash 'source'
rule_summary:
**What was fixed:** Replaced `source` with `.` in `scripts/backup-to-supabase.sh`.  

**Why it broke:** `source` is a Bash built-in, not POSIX-compliant. When the script was invoked with `/bin/sh` (wh
lesson_summary:
**What was fixed:** Replaced `source` with `.` in `scripts/backup-to-supabase.sh`.  

**Why it broke:** `source` is a Bash built-in, not POSIX-compliant. When the script was invoked with `/bin/sh` (which may be Dash or another POSIX shell on systems like Debian/Ubuntu), `source` was unrecognized, ca

title: [workflowautomation] fix: add .gitattributes for LF line endings, fix shell script POSIX compatibility
rule_summary:
**What was fixed:**  
Shell script POSIX compatibility issues caused by CRLF line endings in `.mjs` files, and missing `.gitattributes` to enforce consistent LF line endings across the repo.

**Why it
lesson_summary:
**What was fixed:**  
Shell script POSIX compatibility issues caused by CRLF line endings in `.mjs` files, and missing `.gitattributes` to enforce consistent LF line endings across the repo.

**Why it broke:**  
Windows Git clients default to `core.autocrlf=true`, converting LF to CRLF on checkout.

title: [quotation-automation] fix: deploy to wrong VPS - Tailscale IP routes to wrong server

lesson_summary: Deployed workflow page to wrong VPS (104.248.225.250 / SuperRoo Cloud Dashboard) instead of correct project VPS (165.22.110.111 / track.abcx124.xyz). Root cause: used Tailscale IP (100.64.175.88) which routes to SuperRoo VPS. Fix: created learningworkflow.md, updated README.md, added banner to deploy-agent.mjs, reverted nginx on wrong VPS.

title: Telegram Markdown filename escaping
rule_summary: Telegram Markdown parse errors can be triggered by uploaded filenames like photo_123.jpg when interpolated into messages with parse_mode Markdown. Escape user/file/API dynamic text before using Markdo
lesson_summary: Telegram Markdown parse errors can be triggered by uploaded filenames like photo_123.jpg when interpolated into messages with parse_mode Markdown. Escape user/file/API dynamic text before using Markdown, or omit parse_mode for error messages that include raw exception text.

**Why it broke:**  
The code was overwriting localStorage with default accounts instead of merging them into the existing stored data

Telegram enforces that only one webhook can be act

A 409 Conflict error on bot restart caused by an existing webhook not being deleted before `bot.launch()`.

Telegram enforces that only one webhook can be active per bot at a time. On restart, the previous webhook was still registered, so calling `bot.launch
